title: Star Unpacking
description: Master the star (*) unpacking operator for tuples. Write functions that decompose a tuple into its head and tail, extract just the first and last elements, drop the minimum and maximum values from a sorted tuple, and partition a tuple into two parts at a given index using extended unpacking.
starterCode:
def head_tail(t):
    """Return a tuple (head, tail) where head is the first element
    and tail is a tuple of the remaining elements.
    Use star unpacking."""
    pass


def first_last(t):
    """Return a tuple of just the first and last elements.
    Use star unpacking to ignore the middle."""
    pass


def drop_extremes(t):
    """Given a sorted numeric tuple, return a tuple with
    the smallest and largest values removed.
    Use star unpacking."""
    pass


def partition(t, n):
    """Split tuple t into two tuples at index n.
    Return (left, right) where left = t[:n] and right = t[n:]."""
    pass
hints: Star unpacking uses the * operator: head, *tail = (1, 2, 3) assigns head=1 and tail=[2, 3].
Use _ as a throwaway variable name: first, *_, last = t captures only the first and last elements.
Remember that *rest captures a list, so you need tuple() to convert it back to a tuple.
Partition can be done with simple slicing: t[:n] and t[n:].

testCases.description: partition at index 0 returns empty left tuple
